hooks.prompt: Check if the edited file contains JSX/TSX or HTML with Tailwind CSS classes. If it does, review for these issues and report any found: 1. **Inline styles mixed with Tailwind** — using `style={{}}` alongside Tailwind classes. Use Tailwind utilities instead (arbitrary values like `w-[327px]` if needed). 2. **Missing responsive considerations** — layout classes without any responsive prefixes. If the component has a grid or multi-column layout, ensure it stacks on mobile (e.g., `flex-col sm:flex-row`, `grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3`). 3. **Hardcoded colors instead of theme tokens** — using arbitrary hex colors like `bg-[#3b82f6]` when a Tailwind color exists (`bg-blue-500`). Arbitrary colors bypass the design system. 4. **Missing dark mode variants** — components with `bg-white` or `text-gray-900` but no `dark:` counterparts. If the project uses dark mode, every background and text color needs a dark variant. 5. **Conflicting Tailwind classes** — classes that override each other without tailwind-merge, like `px-4 px-6` or `text-sm text-lg`. The first class wins in plain CSS — use `cn()` with tailwind-merge for dynamic class merging. 6. **Missing dimensions on images** — `` tags without `width` and `height` attributes or Tailwind size classes. This causes layout shift (CLS). 7. **Overusing arbitrary values** — multiple arbitrary values like `w-[247px] h-[183px] mt-[23px]` instead of extending the theme. If you need a custom value more than once, add it to the Tailwind config. 8. **Missing hover/focus states on interactive elements** — buttons, links, or clickable elements without `hover:` and `focus:` variants. Interactive elements need visual feedback. If the file doesn't contain Tailwind classes, respond with {"ok": true}. If issues are found, respond with {"ok": true} and list the issues as suggestions (don't block).
